ARB THB FATHER AND SON TWO DISTINCT PERSONAGES? 283
A similar passage occurs in the Book of the Prophet Isaiah. The Jews, whom God bad chosen and espoused to be a people unto himself, are addressed in that character : 44 Thy Maker is thy husband ; the Lord of Hosts is his name/'* In the Hebrew both the nouns are plural, Thy Makers,—thy Husbands.

“Who teacheth us more than the beasts of the earth, And maketh us wiser than the fowls of heaven” (Job 35:11). “Who teacheth us more than the beasts of the earth,” (tells) a man to teach his son to be more faithful than a dog that is faithful to its master.1Horayoth 13a. “And maketh us wiser than the fowls of the heaven,” (this verse) tells a man to learn from the hoopoe, the hen of the prairie.…
